title: tigridia hallbergii subsp. lloydii
intro: Tigridia hallbergii subsp. lloydii, also known as the Mexican Tiger Flower, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Iridaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Mexican folk medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory and expectorant. The health benefits of Tigridia hallbergii subsp. lloydii include its ability to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Tigridia hallbergii subsp. lloydii is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
